name: Form Configuration Anti-Patterns
summary: Common mistakes and pitfalls to avoid when generating JSON Forms configuration

schemaAntiPatterns.name: UI Schema Scope Mismatch
schemaAntiPatterns.description: UI schema Control references a property that doesn't exist in the data schema
schemaAntiPatterns.explanation: The scope `#/properties/first_name` uses snake_case but the data schema property is `firstName` (camelCase). Scopes must exactly match property names.

schemaAntiPatterns.name: Missing Scope Prefix
schemaAntiPatterns.explanation: Every Control scope must be a JSON pointer starting with `#/properties/`. Omitting the prefix causes the control to not render.

schemaAntiPatterns.name: Non-camelCase Property Names
schemaAntiPatterns.description: Using snake_case, PascalCase, or spaces in data schema property names
schemaAntiPatterns.explanation: Always use camelCase for property names. This is the convention for JSON Forms and ensures consistency in scope references.

schemaAntiPatterns.name: Missing Pages Variant on Categorization
schemaAntiPatterns.description: Using Categorization without specifying the pages variant
schemaAntiPatterns.explanation: For multi-page forms, always set `options.variant` to `"pages"` on the Categorization element. Without it, pages won't render as steps.

schemaAntiPatterns.name: Required Field Not in Properties
schemaAntiPatterns.description: Listing a field in `required` that doesn't exist as a property
schemaAntiPatterns.explanation: Every field listed in `required` must also exist in `properties`. An orphaned required field causes validation errors.

schemaAntiPatterns.name: Including formDefinitionId in Tool Input
schemaAntiPatterns.description: Passing formDefinitionId as input to formConfigurationUpdateTool
schemaAntiPatterns.explanation: formDefinitionId comes from request context automatically. Including it in tool input causes an error. Only pass name, dataSchema, uiSchema, anonymousApply, applicantRoles, and assessorRoles.

schemaAntiPatterns2.name: Required String Without minLength Workaround
schemaAntiPatterns2.description: Marking a string field as required but not adding `minLength: 1` — empty strings pass validation
schemaAntiPatterns2.explanation: JSON Forms has a known bug where an empty string satisfies the `required` rule. Always add `minLength: 1` to required string fields as a workaround.

schemaAntiPatterns2.name: SHOW/HIDE Rule Without Conditional Validation
schemaAntiPatterns2.description: Using a SHOW/HIDE rule on a required field without adding if/then conditional validation in the data schema

bad.uiSchema_note: childsName has SHOW rule based on hasChildren, but childsName is always required in data schema

schemaAntiPatterns2.explanation: Hidden required fields cause form validation to fail silently because the user cannot fill them in. Always pair SHOW/HIDE rules with if/then conditional required in the data schema.

schemaAntiPatterns2.name: Nested Object Scope Missing Full Path
schemaAntiPatterns2.description: Using a flat scope for a nested object property instead of the full JSON pointer path

bad.note: When firstName is nested under a 'name' object

schemaAntiPatterns2.explanation: When data schema uses nested objects (e.g., `name.firstName`), UI schema scopes must include the full path. Using `#/properties/firstName` when the property is at `properties.name.properties.firstName` will not render.

schemaAntiPatterns2.name: Rule Condition Scope Points to Wrong Field
schemaAntiPatterns2.description: The rule's condition scope references a non-existent or wrong property
schemaAntiPatterns2.explanation: If the condition scope resolves to undefined, JSON Schema validation succeeds and the condition evaluates to true — the rule always fires. Make sure the condition scope matches an actual property name in the data schema.

designAntiPatterns.name: Too Many Fields on a Single Page
designAntiPatterns.description: Putting 6+ fields in a VerticalLayout without pagination
designAntiPatterns.solution: Use Categorization with `variant: "pages"` when you have 6 or more fields. Group related fields into separate Category pages.

designAntiPatterns.name: No Help Text on Complex Fields
designAntiPatterns.description: Omitting contextual help for fields that need explanation
designAntiPatterns.solution: Add `options.help` to Controls for fields where users might be uncertain what to enter. Use HelpContent elements for section-level guidance.

designAntiPatterns.name: Including Full JSON in Chat Response
designAntiPatterns.description: Showing the entire data/UI schema JSON to the user instead of applying it
designAntiPatterns.solution: Apply changes using formConfigurationUpdateTool and describe what changed in plain language. Only show JSON if the user explicitly asks for it.

designAntiPatterns.name: Missing FOIP Notice on Government Forms
designAntiPatterns.description: Government forms that collect personal information without a privacy/FOIP collection notice
designAntiPatterns.solution: Add a HelpContent with markdown containing the Notice of Collection under FOIP Act Section 33(c). Place it on the final page before the declaration checkbox.

designAntiPatterns.name: Using Default Checkbox for Yes/No Questions
designAntiPatterns.description: Rendering a yes/no question as a plain checkbox instead of Yes/No radio buttons
designAntiPatterns.solution: Use `options.format: "radio"` with `textForTrue: "Yes"` and `textForFalse: "No"` for clear binary questions. Plain checkboxes are ambiguous — unchecked could mean 'no' or 'not yet answered'.

designAntiPatterns.name: Using Dropdown for 2-3 Options
designAntiPatterns.description: Using a dropdown menu when there are only 2-3 options — radio buttons are clearer
designAntiPatterns.solution: Use `options.format: "radio"` for 2-3 options so all choices are visible without clicking. Reserve dropdowns for 4+ options.

designAntiPatterns.name: Using Deprecated HelpContent Variants (hyperlink/img)
designAntiPatterns.description: Using `variant: hyperlink` or `variant: img` on HelpContent — these are deprecated
designAntiPatterns.solution: Use markdown HelpContent instead. For links: `{ "markdown": true, "help": ["[Link text](url)"] }`. For images: `{ "markdown": true, "help": ["![Alt text](image-url)"] }`.
